concerns.text: The 'Assumptions' section contains a full Functional Requirement (FR) disguised as an assumption. It states: 'The system MUST utilize a CPU-optimized diffusion model... If the specific model architecture only permits approximate seed control... the system MUST implement a fallback mechanism...'. This contradicts the section header 'Assumptions' (which should list external dependencies or beliefs) and duplicates the scope of FR-003. It should be moved to 'Functional Requirements' or split into a new FR (e.g., FR-007) to maintain terminology stability and logical separation.

In US-2 Acceptance Scenario 1, the text reads: 'Then it must produce [deferred] images (500 per group)...'. The use of '[deferred]' here is inconsistent with the surrounding context which explicitly defines the dataset size as 500 in US-1 and the 'Expected results' section. While the spec template encourages deferring empirical values, the number 500 is already established as a fixed parameter of the study design in multiple places. Using '[deferred]' here creates a terminological conflict with the established scope of '500 scenes' defined in US-1 and the 'Data Curation' step in the Idea Md.

The entity 'ViolationRecord' is defined as 'A boolean flag and metadata...'. However, FR-004 and SC-001 refer to calculating a 'violation rate' (a percentage/aggregate metric) rather than a per-image boolean record. While a boolean record is a valid implementation detail, the terminology 'ViolationRecord' implies a singular entity, whereas the success criteria and requirements focus on the aggregate 'rate'. Consider renaming the entity to 'ViolationInstance' or 'GeometricViolation' to better align with the aggregate nature of the 'violation rate' metric described in FR-004 and SC-001.
